name: Shell Town
description: Shell Town is a small coastal town in the East Blue dominated by Marine Base 153. Once a peaceful settlement, it fell under the tyrannical rule of Captain 'Axe-Hand' Morgan, who terrorized citizens with his ego and cruelty. The town became famous as the place where Roronoa Zoro joined Monkey D. Luffy's crew.
history: Shell Town was a quiet marine outpost until Captain Morgan rose to power. His brutal rule turned the base into a symbol of fear rather than justice. He forced citizens to build a massive statue of himself while punishing anyone who opposed him. His reign ended when Luffy and Zoro exposed his corruption and defeated him, restoring peace to the town. Coby stayed behind to pursue his dream of becoming a marine under proper leadership.
culture: Shell Town's culture was suppressed under Morgan's rule, with citizens living in fear of arbitrary punishment. The town traditionally valued hard work and community, but Morgan's ego cult replaced normal life. After his defeat, the town slowly recovered its identity as a peaceful maritime community. The marine base, under new leadership, now serves its proper purpose of protecting citizens.
transportation: Small fishing vessels and marine ships dock at the harbor. The marine base controls ship traffic. Within town, travel is on foot through cobblestone streets.

traditions: Rika's mother makes special rice balls
quirks: Rice balls symbolize kindness in the face of tyranny

notablePeople.name: Roronoa Zoro
notablePeople.description: The legendary swordsman who was captured while protecting a girl from Morgan's son. He joined Luffy's crew after being freed and defeating Morgan's men.

notablePeople.name: Monkey D. Luffy
notablePeople.description: The rubber man who freed Zoro and defeated Captain Morgan, recruiting his first crew member.

notablePeople.name: Captain Morgan
notablePeople.description: The corrupt, egomaniacal marine captain with an axe for a hand who ruled Shell Town through fear and oppression.

notablePeople.name: Helmeppo
notablePeople.description: Morgan's cowardly son who abused his father's power. After Morgan's defeat, he reformed and became a proper marine under Garp's training.

notablePeople.name: Coby
notablePeople.description: The timid boy who became Luffy's first friend. He stayed at Shell Town to pursue his dream of becoming a marine admiral.

notablePeople.name: Rika
notablePeople.description: The brave young girl who tried to feed Zoro rice balls while he was tied up, showing kindness despite the risk.

notablePeople.name: Ripper
notablePeople.description: Morgan's second-in-command who followed orders out of fear but was secretly relieved when Morgan was defeated.

subLocations.name: Marine Base 153
subLocations.description: The imposing marine fortress that dominates Shell Town. Under Morgan, it became a symbol of oppression.
notableFeatures: Execution grounds where Zoro was tied

subLocations.name: Town Square
subLocations.description: The central gathering place where citizens witnessed Zoro's capture and eventual liberation.

subLocations.name: Rika's Restaurant
subLocations.description: A small family-run restaurant where Rika and her mother serve food to locals and occasionally marines.

items.name: Zoro's Swords
items.description: The three swords Zoro recovered after being freed.

items.name: Rice Balls
items.description: The rice balls Rika made for Zoro, symbolizing the kindness that still existed under Morgan's tyranny.

items.name: Morgan's Axe-Hand
items.description: The massive axe attached to Morgan's arm, a symbol of his brutality.

factions.name: Shell Town Citizens
factions.description: The townspeople who suffered under Morgan's rule but found hope in Luffy's actions.

factions.name: Morgan's Marines
factions.description: Marines who followed Morgan out of fear. Most were relieved when he was defeated.

factions.name: Reformed Marines
factions.description: The same marines, now serving under proper leadership and protecting citizens as they should.

events: Luffy and Coby arrive in Shell Town
Luffy meets Zoro tied up at the marine base
Rika brings rice balls to Zoro
Helmeppo reveals his plan to execute Zoro
Luffy frees Zoro from his bonds
Zoro officially joins the Straw Hat Pirates
Coby stays to become a marine

quickFacts: Where Zoro joined the Straw Hat Pirates
Where Coby began his marine career
Home to the famous rice ball incident
First major victory for the Straw Hats

videos.title: Luffy Meets Zoro
videos.description: The first meeting between Luffy and his future first mate, tied up at the marine base.

videos.title: Zoro Joins Luffy's Crew
videos.description: After being freed, Zoro officially becomes the first member of Luffy's pirate crew.
